intro.title: Hello, I'm Zhengbing He
intro.desc: Professor in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems. University of Nottingham Ningbo China. As a principal investigator, I have secured funding exceeding $1 million for national-level (key) projects. I have published 180+ academic papers, including 50+ published exclusively in the prestigious journal series of Transportation Research and IEEE Transactions. I am proud that I have published papers across the entire Transportation Research family (TR A/B/C/D/E/F), 20+ TRC and 10+ TITS. And, a Correspondence in Nature.Total citations: 8000+, i10-index: 100+, H-index: 40+. I am an IEEE senior member and was listed as World’s Top 2% Scientists, ranking 67/30,588 in Transportation & Logistics (23,536 in all fields). I serve as a Senior Editor for IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems, a Deputy Editor-in-Chief for IET Intelligent Transport Systems, an Associate Editor for IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Vehicles, an Editorial Advisory Board member for Transportation Research Part C, etc.My research lies at the intersection of urban mobility, systems engineering, and artificial intelligence, spanning from traditional topics such as traffic flow operations and control, sustainability, and resilience to emerging areas including data-driven modeling, autonomous driving, and large language models. In particular, I am a pioneer and long-term contributor in AI-driven transportation modeling and AV-empowered traffic mitigation, and an early innovator in generative AI applications in transportation (see Scientific Contributions below).

item0.title: Pioneer and long-term contributor to AI-based transportation modeling
item0.desc: Car-following and lane-changing models are cornerstones for understanding driver behavior, traffic dynamics, and supporting autonomous driving. As early as 2014, at the dawn of the deep learning era, my team proposed an innovative kNN-based data-driven car-following model. This model has been cited almost 200 times, and it is widely recognized as one of the first data-driven car-following models by review articles published by scholars from institutions such as the University of California, Berkeley (Zhang et al., 2025), the University of Washington (Chen et al., 2023), and Imperial College London (Rowam et al., 2025). In 2019, my team developed the first deep-learning-based lane-changing model using DBN and LSTM neural networks, which has since been cited over 300 times and is widely recognized in subsequent research (Siebke et al., 2023; Han et al., 2024; Wang et al., 2024; Huang et al., 2025) as a pioneering contribution to AI-driven modeling of driver behavior and traffic dynamics.Prediction is a central topic in transportation research and practice. I contributed to the development of the first CNN-based network-wide traffic speed prediction model, and the single paper has been cited over 1,600 times. Other representative works include spatial-temporal graph attention transformer-based trajectory prediction, graph CNN-based bike-sharing demand prediction, GAN-based travel time prediction, etc. All these works were published in leading journals, such as TRC, CACAIE, and Information Fusion, and have received substantial citations, making a significant impact on the field of AI-driven transportation prediction.

item1.title: An early innovator in large language model (LLM) applications in transportation.
item1.desc: Since the emergence of LLMs in 2023, my team was among the first to introduce LLM-based intelligent driving assistance, highlight the positive role of prompt engineering in enhancing prediction accuracy, and develop AI agent models for everyday route choice, with a series of early papers published in leading journals. These works rank among the earliest applications of LLMs and have attracted wide attention in the research community. In 2024, I was twice invited to introduce those LLM-based progresses in ground mobility at SAE International, the world’s leading authority on automotive industry standards. At the beginning of 2026, we had a Correspondence, titled

item2.title: Developed autonomous driving strategies to mitigate traffic congestion, shaping the field
item2.desc: To address highway congestion and emissions, my team was the first to propose a feedback control–based congestion mitigation strategy for connected vehicles and the jam-absorption driving strategy, with results published in IEEE TITS and more than 300 citations and reported by Italian media.  Researchers in Japan cited our work on jam-absorption strategies more than ten times in a single paper (Nishi, 2020), recognizing the two-step jam-absorption strategy as an effective means of alleviating traffic congestion. I am leading a survey paper co-authored with scholars from MIT, Georgia Tech, and TU Delft, which is forthcoming in IEEE TITS, reflecting my international influence in this topic.For the urban scenarios, I introduced a novel future no-lane-change road transportation system for autonomous driving and subsequently extended the framework to include pedestrians. The series of works was published in IEEE TIV, IEEE TVT, and TRC, drawing wide attention from the research community (such as more than 100,000 views on LinkedIn). Researchers from institutions such as the University of Florida provided a detailed and positive assessment of the no-lane-change concept (Li et al., 2018), and subsequent studies by Antonio et al. (2022) and Zhao et al. (2025) considered it one of the first conflict-based intersection management.I have also devoted significant effort to predicting the motion of surrounding agents for autonomous vehicles, with a particular focus on intersection conflict scenarios. To date, I have developed a series of methods, including approaches based on Transformers, LLMs with CoT reasoning, and World Models.

item3.title: Advanced traffic state sensing and visualization, driving practical applications
item3.desc: Time-space traffic diagrams are one of the most important traffic analysis and visualization tools. The series of works centered on the enhancement of the diagrams, including the mapping-to-cell approaches, the wave-aware construction, the traffic emission estimation and the resolution enhancement. In particular, the wave-aware method of constructing time-space diagrams has been substantively implemented in the well-known I-24 MOTION project in Nashville (Ji et al., 2024; Ji et al., 2026), as well as in the works of global researchers from the UK (Shang et al., 2023), Sweden (Tsanakas et al., 2022), and China (He et al., 2024). Notably, these implementations were carried out completely independently of our own research activities.

item4.title: Also active in resilience, sustainability, shared mobility, and travel behavior
item4.desc: Representative works include the assessment of the dynamic vulnerability of transportation systems, a mesoscopic CO2 emission estimation model with the consideration of traffic dynamics, multi-day rail-hailing behavior characteristics and modeling, and in-lab experiment-based day-to-day route choice modeling, demonstrating my broad research interests, strong research skills, effective team leadership and collaboration, and wide-reaching impact.I co-authored multiple highly cited review articles in transportation research, synthesizing trajectory data studies (citation>250), automated vehicle-involved traffic (citation>250), and transportation system resilience (citation>200). These reviews have guided subsequent research, reflecting both scholarly recognition and field-wide impact.

item5.title: Intelligent Rice Transplanting and Harvesting Robot
item5.desc: FreeRTOS-based agricultural robot for rice transplanting and harvesting. Controlled DJI 3508 motors for gripper lifting and an omnidirectional chassis, achieving ±5 mm positioning accuracy. Participated in pneumatic design and hardware wiring, supporting dual-robot collaboration.

item6.title: SliverWolf Desktop Quadruped and Arm
item6.desc: An open-source desktop 12-DOF serial-link quadruped with an optional robotic arm and gripper. Built around STM32F103 and MG92R micro servos, the repository includes body and arm STL files, PCB PDFs, quadruped firmware, voice relay firmware, posture/button-control examples, and an LD3320 voice-recognition project. Serial commands cover standing, forward/backward walking, turning, strafing, crawling, handshaking, light effects, and one-click object fetching through Bluetooth, IMU/button, and voice-control paths.

item7.title: 3508 Legged-Robot Drive Test Platform
item7.desc: A DJI M3508-powered quadruped drive validation platform iterated by the MTI Robotics Team at Fujian University of Technology since 2022. Starting from an F427 ROBOCON chassis architecture, it evolved into a quadruped control program with RC/keyboard operation, variable PID motor parameters, stable standing, gyroscope attitude balance, LiDAR height holding, gait timing, frame compensation, backward gait, turning, and track testing for multiple ROBOCON legged-robot seasons.

item8.title: ROBOCON2024-PPS
item8.desc: MTIPPS is a planar base-axis positioning system for ROBOCON 2024 robots. Based on STM32F103C8T6, RS485 absolute encoders, and omni-wheel code disks, it reads XY displacement with millimeter-level travel recording while collecting Hi219 gyroscope attitude data. The system streams positioning and attitude information to the robot mainboard for chassis localization, path-planning, and autonomous driving tests.

item9.title: Typst Chinese Resume Template Library
item9.desc: A Typst resume template library that can be edited directly and exported to PDF. It includes basic resume layouts, multiple reference-style templates, exported PDFs, preview images, and optional avatar assets. The workflow is built around VS Code with the Tinymist Typst extension for live preview, with README notes covering Typst CLI compilation, batch export, and font dependency setup.

item0.title: Robotics Engineer Learning Roadmap (Preview) - Lain-Ego on Zhihu
item0.desc: A structured learning path for robotics engineers, covering core areas such as motion control, perception, embedded systems, and SLAM, with phased study suggestions and resource indexing.

item1.title: A Guide to Career Paths in the Robotics Industry — for High School Students - KKR Database on Zhihu
item1.desc: An overview of development paths and subfields in the robotics industry for high school students, helping connect university major choices with career directions through a clear industry landscape.

item2.title: On the Organizational Maturity of University Robotics Competition Teams - KKR Database on Zhihu
item2.desc: Exploring how university robotics competition teams can improve organizational efficiency, knowledge transfer, and collaboration — from team structure and document management to sustainable season planning.

event11.title: Internship at Qianxing Intelligence
event11.desc: Responsible for motion control and human-robot interaction R&D for humanoid robots.

event10.title: Launched LocoWiki Resource Hub
event10.desc: Launched the LocoWiki resource hub for robot competition preparation, research learning, and development reference, bringing together competition rules, technical sharing, paper reading lists, and high-quality open-source projects in one place.

event12.title: Launched OpenDoge Open-Source Quadruped Crowdfunding
event12.desc: Participated as a founding developer in the OpenDoge quadruped robot crowdfunding campaign, leading algorithm and hardware driver deployment layer R&D. Project delivery scheduled for late June 2026.

event1.title: Open-Sourced SliverWolf Desktop Serial-Link Quadruped
event1.desc: Released SliverWolf, a desktop serial-link quadruped robot featuring voice control and robotic-arm collaboration.

event2.title: 2024 ROBOCON National Competition
event2.desc: Developed the embedded control system for the R1 robot based on FreeRTOS, achieving ±5mm positioning accuracy, ball launching, and gripper functionalities.

event4.title: Fujian Intelligent Marine Equipment Design Competition
event4.desc: Led control-system development for a buoyancy-controllable multi-DOF lobster-inspired underwater robot, winning the Grand Prize.

event5.title: 2024 World Marine Equipment Conference
event5.desc: Represented Fujian University of Technology as an exhibitor, showcasing the bionic lobster robot; covered by media outlets including Xinhua News Agency.

event6.title: 2025 ROBOCON National Competition
event6.desc: Responsible for full-stack R&D of the legged robot. Ranked 30th nationally in the Speed Race, 31st in the Obstacle Race, and 34th in the Cross-Country Race, securing National Second Prize in all three categories.

event7.title: 2025 National Undergraduate Electronics Design Contest
event7.desc: Responsible for the hardware design and fabrication of the power meter for an automatic ranging system, winning the National Second Prize.

event8.title: Open-Sourced BRS Parallel-Link Quadruped Robot
event8.desc: Open-sourced the mechanical structure and control code of the BRS parallel-link quadruped robot on GitHub.

event9.title: Internship at HighTorque Robotics
event9.desc: Responsible for mechanical design, control algorithm development, and system integration testing for the HTDW4438 bionic quadruped robot, covering both open-source and productized versions.
